frameworks.name: non-instrumental information-seeking
frameworks.one_line: Effort exerted to obtain information that cannot change the outcome.

frameworks.name: risk vs ambiguity distinction
frameworks.one_line: Separates known-probability uncertainty from unknown-probability uncertainty.

frameworks.name: valence-uncertainty account of information demand
frameworks.one_line: Valence and uncertainty as drivers of curiosity, tested in effort domain.

core_question: Does physical effort exerted for non-instrumental information depend on valence and on the type of uncertainty (risk vs ambiguity)?
key_assumptions: Incentive-compatible physical effort indexes the subjective value of information
Risk and ambiguity are dissociable forms of uncertainty
Information has value even when non-instrumental for the decision
manipulations: Type of uncertainty: risk vs ambiguity
Magnitude of outcome distribution uncertainty within each type
key_findings: Willingness to exert effort positively correlates with expected value under both risk and ambiguity
More effort exerted as outcome distribution uncertainty increases under risk
Insensitivity to ambiguity level, except at extreme ambiguity
interpretations: Effort-based information demand for non-instrumental information dissociates between risk and ambiguity
Humans engage effortful information-seeking under risk but largely not to resolve ambiguity
